title: NOC 65102 – Store shelf stockers, clerks and order fillers | GTR Immigration
description: Store shelf stockers, clerks, and order fillers under NOC 65102 are the logistical backbone of Canada’s retail and e-commerce infrastructure. As a TEER 5 occupation, this role has transformed in 2026 into a high-tech position involving inventory management software and rapid digital order fulfillment. Whether working in massive "Big Box" retailers or specialized fulfillment centers, these professionals ensure that supply chains remain fluid. For immigration, this role is a vital entry point through the Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP) and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), offering a stable path to Permanent Residency (PR) for those ready to enter the Canadian workforce.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level5Major Group65 – Sales and service support occupationsSub-Major Group651 – Sales and service support occupationsMinor Group6510 – Sales and service support occupationsNOC Code65102

duties: Pick and pack merchandise from warehouse or storage areas to fill digital and in-store orders.
Stock shelves and display areas with merchandise and maintain organized aisles.
Use handheld scanners and computers to track inventory levels and process price changes.
Attach protective devices (security tags) and price tags to merchandise.
Assist customers in locating items within the store.
Unpack merchandise received from suppliers and verify against invoices.
Keep stock rooms and sales floors clean and safe for both staff and customers.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided for all new staff.
Basic literacy and the ability to use handheld digital devices are mandatory.
High proficiency in English or French (or both) is required for safety and coordination.
Physical stamina and the ability to lift moderate weights are essential for this role.
job_outlook: The outlook for this occupation is Favourable. As retail transitions further into a "hybrid" model of physical and digital sales, the need for human order fillers to manage complex, non-automated picking tasks remains high. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), the sector will continue to provide a high volume of entry-level openings due to industry growth and workforce turnover.
labour_market_demand: The demand for order fillers and stockers is Very Good, driven largely by the continued dominance of e-commerce and "Click-and-Collect" shopping habits. In 2026, major demand is concentrated in logistics hubs like Peel Region (Ontario), Calgary (Alberta), and the Lower Mainland (BC). Retailers are actively seeking workers to manage the "last-mile" fulfillment process, making this one of the most consistently available roles for newcomers.
High Accessibility: Minimal formal education requirements allow you to secure employment and begin your Canadian journey immediately.
Tech-Forward Experience: Gain experience with modern inventory systems and automated fulfillment technology.
Physical Activity: Ideal for those who prefer a dynamic, active work environment over a desk-bound role.
Growth Potential: Direct pathways to move into Retail Supervision (NOC 62010) or Logistics Coordination (NOC 12013).
pathways: Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A primary route for TEER 5 workers with a job offer from a designated employer in the Atlantic provinces.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): A dedicated pathway for those working in retail or tourism for at least 9 months in British Columbia.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track PR for those currently working in Alberta on a valid work permit in eligible sectors.
Rural and Northern Immigration Pilot (RNIP): Addressing critical labor needs in participating smaller communities across the country.
